domain: Reinforcement Learning for SLM Reasoning (GRPO/RLVR Tools)

tools.name: TRL (Transformers Reinforcement Learning)
tools.description: Full-stack library for post-training transformer LMs with RL methods including SFT, GRPO, DPO, PPO, reward modeling. Built on HF Transformers ecosystem.

tools.name: Unsloth
tools.description: Memory-efficient fine-tuning and RL training framework. Enables GRPO training with as low as 5GB VRAM for 1.5B models. Supports QLoRA/LoRA for GRPO.

tools.name: OpenRLHF
tools.description: Scalable and high-performance agentic RL framework based on Ray, vLLM, DeepSpeed. Only 8,523 LOC. Adopted by Google, ByteDance, NVIDIA, Tencent, etc.

tools.name: veRL (Volcano Engine RL)
tools.description: Flexible multi-backend RL framework for LLMs. Supports FSDP + vLLM combinations. Large community, actively developed.

tools.name: NeMo-RL
tools.description: Scalable post-training library under NVIDIA NeMo Framework. Ray-based orchestration, vLLM/TensorRT-LLM generation, Megatron-Core support.

tools.name: NeMo-Gym
tools.description: Build RL environments for LLM training. Companion to NeMo-RL for defining reward functions and training environments.

tools.name: ROLL
tools.description: Efficient RL library for LLMs with large-scale GPU support. Multi-role distributed architecture with Ray. Integrates Megatron-Core, SGLang, vLLM.

tools.name: AReaL
tools.description: Fully asynchronous RL training system for large reasoning and agentic models. Focus on lightning-fast RL with simplicity and flexibility.

tools.name: Open-R1
tools.description: Open reproduction of DeepSeek-R1 training pipeline. Focus on data and training transparency for reasoning model development.

tools.name: DeepScaleR
tools.description: Open-source project to democratize RL for LLMs. DeepScaleR-1.5B-Preview surpasses O1-Preview with 43.1% on AIME.

tools.name: Open-Reasoner-Zero (ORZ)
tools.description: First open-source implementation of large-scale reasoning-oriented RL on base models. Achieves superior performance with 1/10 training steps vs DeepSeek-R1-Zero.

tools.name: SimpleRL-Reason
tools.description: Simple RL recipe for reasoning using only rule-based rewards and GSM8K/Math datasets. Trains 10 diverse base models with 8K examples achieving 10-20+ point gains.

tools.name: Open-RS
tools.description: Study on RL for reasoning in small LLMs. Explores enhancing reasoning under resource-constrained conditions. AAAI 2026 paper.

tools.name: DAPO (algorithm/system)
tools.description: Decoupled Clip and Dynamic Sampling Policy Optimization. Achieves 50 points on AIME 2024 with Qwen2.5-32B. Introduces Clip-Higher, Dynamic Sampling, Token-Level Loss, KL removal.

tools.name: Dr-GRPO
tools.description: Removes reward scaling and length bias from GRPO. Recommends not scaling by standard deviation to eliminate question-level difficulty bias.

tools.name: Scaf-GRPO
tools.description: Scaffolded GRPO that provides minimal guidance only when model's independent learning plateaus. Progressive training framework.

tools.name: Guru Dataset
tools.description: Curated RL reasoning corpus of 92K verifiable examples spanning 6 reasoning domains: Math, Code, Science, Logic, Simulation, Tabular.

tools.name: RM-R1
tools.description: Reward Modeling as Reasoning. Chain-of-rubrics (CoR) mechanism for self-generating sample-level rubrics. Two-stage training: distillation + RLVR.

core_algorithms.GRPO: Group Relative Policy Optimization - removes critic/value model from PPO, uses group statistics. DeepSeek R1 foundation.
core_algorithms.PPO: Proximal Policy Optimization - classic RL algorithm with critic model, more memory intensive.
core_algorithms.DPO: Direct Preference Optimization - offline, no reward model needed, preference-based.
core_algorithms.REINFORCE++: Single-sample variant, more token-efficient than GRPO, more stable training.

grpo_variants.DAPO: Decoupled clipping, dynamic sampling, token-level loss, no KL
grpo_variants.Dr-GRPO: Removes reward scaling and length bias
grpo_variants.Scaf-GRPO: Scaffolded progressive training with plateau detection

reward_approaches.Verifiable Rewards (RLVR): Binary correct/wrong from deterministic verifiers - math, code, logic
reward_approaches.Rule-based Rewards: Custom reward functions for format compliance, correctness
reward_approaches.Reward Models: Trained neural networks scoring outputs (traditional RLHF)
reward_approaches.LLM-as-Judge: Using another LLM to score outputs
